subject: Speeding cars on Granville Ave near the school
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I walk my kids down Granville Ave every morning. The school-zone limit is 20 but cars fly through at 35 or 40 — I've clocked them. Yesterday a pickup blew past while kids were in the crosswalk at Granville and 25th. Nobody was hurt but it was terrifying. Can we get a speed camera or at least a flashing beacon on that block? Something has to change before someone gets hit.

subject: Speeding cars on Granville Ave near the school
body_clean: Ms. Szczepanski, thank you for writing. Mayor Okonkwo asked me to let you know he's passed your message to Public Works and our Police Department community liaison. Officer Banks will schedule increased patrols in the Granville and 25th school zone during arrival and dismissal hours, and Public Works will assess whether an additional flashing beacon is warranted. We'll follow up once we have a clearer picture of next steps.

subject: Stop sign needed at Harvard Ave and 19th Ave
body_clean: Hi, I live on Harvard Ave and the intersection at 19th is basically a free-for-all. No stop sign on either approach. Cars come off 19th going north and don't even slow down. There have been at least three fender-benders on that corner since spring and a neighbor's kid on a bike almost got clipped last week. This needs a four-way stop or at the very minimum a yield sign.

subject: Stop sign needed at Harvard Ave and 19th Ave
body_clean: Mr. Thibodeau, Frank DiMeo, Public Works. Mayor forwarded your email. I'm putting Harvard and 19th on the list for a traffic-control warrant study — we look at vehicle volumes, sight lines, and crash history. Takes about three weeks. If the data supports a four-way stop we can have signs installed within 60 days of the study closing. I'll be in touch.

subject: Speed bumps on Eastern Ave between Washington and Monroe
body_clean: Good morning Mayor, I've lived on Eastern Ave for eleven years and the drag-racing situation between Washington Blvd and Monroe St has become genuinely dangerous. Last Saturday night a car went airborne over the dip at the storm drain, landed half on the parkway, and took out a fire hydrant. No injuries, thank God, but this is not the first time. Speed bumps or rumble strips would at least slow people down. Please help.

subject: Dangerous speeding on Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mayor, Marshall Ave between 25th and Mannheim has become a cut-through speedway since the St. Charles construction started. GPS apps route everyone down Marshall and they drive it like a highway — I've timed cars at 45 in a 25 zone. Kids waiting for the school bus at Marshall and 22nd are standing three feet from traffic moving that speed. Can we get temporary no-thru-traffic signs or a speed trailer?

subject: Dangerous speeding on Marshall Ave
body_clean: Mr. Ferrante, thank you — you're describing something several neighbors have flagged and I take it seriously. I've asked Frank DiMeo to deploy our speed-feedback trailer on Marshall Ave this week. Officer Banks will coordinate extra patrols during school-bus hours. If the trailer data confirms what you're seeing, we'll move quickly toward a traffic-calming solution. I appreciate you writing.

subject: Crosswalk at Washington Blvd and Bohland Ave — someone is going to get killed
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I walk along Washington Blvd regularly and the crosswalk at Bohland is completely ignored by drivers. I watched a woman pushing a stroller nearly get hit by a left-turning SUV on Tuesday — the driver didn't even brake. Is there any possibility of a pedestrian-activated signal or at least high-visibility overhead signs at that crossing? It's a heavily walked block.

subject: Requesting a crossing guard at Rice Ave and 25th Ave
body_clean: Dear Mayor, my daughter started kindergarten this fall and her walk to school crosses Rice Ave at 25th. There is no crossing guard and traffic is intense in the mornings — a lot of folks cutting over from Mannheim. Other towns have guards at crossings like this. I'd like to formally request one here. Several other families share this concern and we'd be happy to come to a council meeting if that would help.

subject: Requesting a crossing guard at Rice Ave and 25th Ave
body_clean: Mr. Washington, the Mayor asked me to reach out right away. This request is being shared with the Police Department, who manage crossing-guard assignments, and with Public Works for a traffic-volume review at Rice and 25th. Mayor Okonkwo would welcome you and any interested families at the next Public Safety Committee meeting — October 15th at Village Hall, 6 p.m.

subject: Truck traffic ruining St. Paul Ave
body_clean: Mayor, I have lived on St. Paul Ave for 30 years. This summer commercial trucks have gotten completely out of control. Every day flatbeds and concrete mixers cut through — they are not supposed to be on residential streets like this. Last Tuesday a big rig clipped the stop sign at St. Paul and 22nd and just kept going. The pavement is cracking from the weight. Can the village put up weight-limit signs and actually enforce them?

subject: Near miss at Mannheim and Madison — please contact IDOT
body_clean: Hello, I know Mannheim is IDOT's road but I nearly got T-boned at Mannheim and Madison this morning. My eastbound signal was green and a pickup came through running a very stale red — I swerved onto the shoulder to avoid a crash. I've seen this happen before at that signal. Even if the Village can't repaint the road, can you contact IDOT to get the signal timing reviewed? I'll go to Springfield myself if I have to but hoping you can help.

subject: Near miss at Mannheim and Madison — please contact IDOT
body_clean: Mr. Simmons, I'm very glad you're safe. You're right that Mannheim Road falls under IDOT jurisdiction — the Village does not control those signals — but that doesn't mean we stay quiet. I'm personally reaching out to our IDOT District 1 contact this week to flag the signal-timing concern at Mannheim and Madison. I'll let you know what they say. Please also consider filing an incident report directly with IDOT's District 1 office; constituent contacts carry real weight with them.

subject: Speed bumps needed on Morris Ave
body_clean: Hi Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing about Morris Ave between Frederick and Hirsch. People use it as a shortcut to avoid the light at 25th and it has become a little drag strip. I counted 22 cars in 20 minutes one afternoon going well above the limit. My dog was nearly struck while I walked her on the parkway. I'd very much appreciate speed humps on that stretch — even two or three would break up the speeding. Thank you.

subject: Stop sign request — Englewood Ave at 50th Ave
body_clean: Mayor, the corner of Englewood and 50th has no traffic control and visibility is terrible because of big pine trees on the southwest corner. I've seen two side-swipe accidents there this year. Drivers come off 50th fast and there's no way to see them until they're right on top of you. Even a two-way stop would help enormously. Is there a formal process to request one or should I just keep emailing?

subject: Speeding motorcycles on Bellwood Ave at night
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, every Friday and Saturday night groups of motorcycles race up and down Bellwood Ave between about 10 PM and 2 AM. They're going extremely fast and the noise is incredible. I've called 911 twice and the cars always arrive after the group has moved on. I understand it's hard to catch them, but is there any way to coordinate a more systematic response? I have a baby and this is affecting our whole household.

subject: Visibility problem at Hirsch Ave and Frederick Ave
body_clean: Mayor, the utility pole on the northeast corner of Hirsch and Frederick has always been a slight problem, but a new privacy fence at 2812 Frederick has created a serious combined blind spot. Last week I inched out so far to see northbound traffic that a southbound car had to swerve. I realize this may involve a private fence, but there has to be something the Village can do about sight lines here.

subject: Visibility problem at Hirsch Ave and Frederick Ave
body_clean: Mr. Piotrowski, Sandra Pulaski, Code Enforcement. The Mayor forwarded your email. I'll inspect the fence at 2812 Frederick against Municipal Code Section 10-4-7, which regulates fence height and placement within sight-triangle setbacks. If the fence is within 30 feet of the corner and exceeds 30 inches in height, it's a violation and the property owner will receive notice to modify or remove within 30 days. I'll do the inspection this week and follow up with you directly.

subject: Speeding and cut-through traffic on Geneva Ave
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I live on the 200 block of Geneva Ave and the speeding has exploded since Washington Blvd construction started funneling traffic onto our street. We have a lot of elderly residents and young children here and people are treating it like a connector road at 40-plus mph. I work in urban planning and would be glad to share some volume data with your office. Would the Village consider a speed table or speed-feedback signage?

subject: Crossing guard urgently needed — Madison St at 25th Ave
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor. I am writing on behalf of myself and fourteen other parents at Franklin School. The crossing at Madison and 25th has no guard and morning traffic is chaotic — cars turn right off 25th while kids are in the street. My son was nearly clipped on Wednesday; I saw it happen with my own eyes. The principal agrees there is a problem but says traffic safety is the Village's responsibility. We are asking for a crossing guard or at minimum a uniformed officer from 7:45 to 8:15. We are prepared to attend any meeting necessary.

subject: Crossing guard urgently needed — Madison St at 25th Ave
body_clean: Ms. Oladele, thank you for writing and for organizing the other families. I've asked Officer Banks to station an officer at Madison and 25th starting Monday morning while we work through the longer-term solution. Crossing-guard assignments involve both the PD and the school district and I'm personally reaching out to the superintendent this week to get that conversation started. I'll send you an update by end of the week.

subject: Speed bumps on Avon Ave — it's like a racetrack
body_clean: Mayor, Avon Ave south of Washington has turned into a racetrack this summer. The block is straight and flat and people just open it up. I measured 38 mph with my own radar detector parked in my driveway on a Tuesday afternoon. I'm 71 and have lived here since 1989 and it's never been this bad. Please just put a few speed bumps in and call it done. Other streets in town have them. Why not us?
